reasoning: The rule explicitly mandates the commission of a crime (vandalism) and harm to an innocent third party's property. No reasonable person would defend a requirement to destroy private property as a legitimate condition of membership.

The rule enforces a clear distributive injustice: requiring identical financial contributions ($500/month) while structurally denying access to the primary benefit (weekend courts) based on age. A reasonable person would immediately view paying full price for restricted access—especially when the resource sits unused—as a fundamental breach of fair dealing.
